base.case_title: American Airlines, Inc. v. North American Airlines, Inc.
base.scenario: The Civil Aeronautics Board (CAB) found that North American Airlines' use of the name 'North American' in the air transportation industry caused substantial public confusion and was an unfair method of competition. The CAB ordered North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'

items.text: The CAB had the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'
The CAB did not have the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'

decision_question: Does the Civil Aeronautics Board have the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American'?

A.source_text: The CAB had the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'
A.short_term: The CAB had the authority to immediately order North American Airlines to stop using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'
A.long_term: The CAB had the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American,' which in the long run would significantly erode the company's brand recognition and customer loyalty, eventually leading to a decline in market share and profitability over time.

B.source_text: The CAB did not have the authority to order North American Airlines to cease using the name 'North American Airlines' or any combination of the word 'American.'
